shower.thought: We spend decades building a version of ourselves for other people, then wonder why we feel like strangers to ourselves.
Nostalgia is just grief wearing a smile.
You can't miss something you never experienced, but you absolutely can miss something you imagined.
The worst part about getting older isn't forgetting things—it's remembering things nobody else does.
Confidence is just fear with better marketing.
Most of your embarrassing moments live rent-free in your head while the person involved has completely forgotten them.
We call it "overthinking" when we're alone, but "due diligence" when we're getting paid for it.
Your biggest regret is probably someone else's most forgettable Tuesday.
Time moves faster the older you get because you're comparing each year to a shrinking total.
The moment you accept you might be wrong about something is the moment you become slightly less wrong about everything.
You can't love someone into staying, and you can't logic someone into leaving.
Dead languages are just dialects that ran out of speakers before they could evolve further.
We fear commitment because we've seen what happens when people stop trying—but we fear freedom for the same reason.
Every conversation you'll never have exists in infinite possibility until you choose the words that kill them all.
Boredom might just be your brain's way of telling you that you already understand this thing well enough to do something harder.
The reason we love dogs is because they're terrible at lying—they experience exactly what their faces show.
You've been a different person so many times that your childhood trauma might not even belong to you anymore.
Wealth isn't about having more; it's about wanting less—which is why rich people often seem poorer than poor people.
The saddest part about growing up is realizing your parents were improvising the whole time.
Forgetting someone you loved might hurt less than remembering them wrong.

motivational.thought: Your limitation is not your circumstances. It's your willingness to be uncomfortable. — Unknown
The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. — Chinese Proverb
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Unknown
You're not behind. You're not ahead. You're exactly where your decisions have brought you. — Unknown
The difference between who you are and who you want to be is what you do. — Bill Phillips
Fear is just excitement without the breath. — Unknown
Small daily improvements over time create remarkable results. — Robin Sharma
You don't rise to the level of your goals; you fall to the level of your systems. — Unknown
Courage is not the absence of fear. It's fear that has said its prayers and moved anyway. — Unknown
The obstacle is the way. What stands in the way becomes the way. — Ryan Holiday
Your future self is watching you right now through memories you haven't made yet. — Unknown
Comparison is the thief of joy, but competition with yourself is the fuel of growth. — Unknown
The only person you're destined to become is the one you decide to be. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
What we fear doing most is usually what we most need to do. — Tim Ferriss
You can't hate your way to the life you love. — Unknown
Peace comes not from the absence of struggle, but from acceptance of it. — Unknown
The moment you're ready to quit is usually the moment right before the breakthrough. — Unknown
Excellence is not a skill, it's an attitude. — Ralph Marston
Your excuses are someone else's opportunities. — Unknown
The quality of your life is determined by the quality of the questions you ask yourself. — Tony Robbins

darkjoke.thought:
My dad told me he was proud of me right before he died.
I think he was just being nice. The bar was pretty low.
I've been on so many dating apps, I'm pretty sure I've already matched with my future ex-wife.

My therapist asked if I'm afraid of commitment.
I said no, but I'll get back to her on that.

I love when people say "let me know if you need anything."
Finally, someone's giving me permission to ignore them.

My financial advisor told me I need to think about my future.
I told him I'm already thinking about my future—it's very concerning.

I signed up for a pottery class to find my passion.
Turns out my passion is leaving hobbies unfinished. At least I'm consistent.

My mom asked why I don't call her anymore.
I told her that's not true—I call her every time my car makes a weird noise.

I tried to be more positive this year.
It didn't work, but I'm failing with better posture.

My friend said he's cutting back on drinking for his health.
I said, "To what—moderately catastrophic?"

I told my cat I had a rough day at work.
He didn't care, which was honestly the emotional support I needed.

My dating profile says I'm "fluent in sarcasm."
Turns out that's not an attractive quality, it's just a personality disorder with good grammar.

My boss asked why I look miserable at work.
I said, "Would you prefer I fake happiness while slowly dying inside?"
He said yes.

I got ghosted by someone I wasn't even dating.
That takes a special kind of effort, and I respect it.

My doctor said I need to exercise more.
I told him I already do—I run from my problems, swim in debt, and walk a fine line.

I asked my friend why he doesn't have a girlfriend.
He said he's "focusing on himself."
Translation: he's very attractive and has low standards for his own behavior.

My barber asked how I wanted my hair cut.
I said, "In a way that distracts from my face."
He said, "I'm not a magician."

I applied for a job that required "passionate self-starters."
I interpreted that as "we won't pay you enough to care."

My family said I should stop making everything about myself.
I couldn't hear them over the sound of my own relevance.

I told my landlord the heater's broken.
He said, "At least you have walls."
He's technically not wrong, which is the most depressing part.

My therapist said I'm defensive.
I said no, I'm not, and asked why she would attack me like that.
